sand_water.description: Sand absorbs water to become mud

sand.note: Cellular automata with 8-connectivity have natural 45-deg angle

tnt.note: Cellular automata with 8-connectivity have natural 45-deg angle

dirt.note: Cellular automata with 8-connectivity have natural 45-deg angle

snow.note: Cellular automata with 8-connectivity have natural 45-deg angle

ash.note: Cellular automata with 8-connectivity have natural 45-deg angle

mass.principle: In a closed system with no reactions, total cell count is constant

energy.principle: Without heat sources/sinks, total thermal energy is constant
energy.note: Integer rounding in heat transfer causes inherent dissipation

momentum.principle: In a symmetric system, net horizontal momentum should be zero
momentum.note: Symmetric sand drop should preserve zero net horizontal momentum

torricelli.note: Ratio between heights is key: v(h1)/v(h2) = sqrt(h1/h2)

pressure_depth.note: Our model is linear by construction

mixed_metal_water.note: Engine uses cell-count weighting (no specific heat capacity)

angle_of_repose.note: Cellular automata with 8-connectivity have a natural 45-deg bias

viscosity.note: Real lava is 100000x more viscous than water; our 4:1 ratio is a game-feel compression

connected_vessels.principle: Water seeks same level in connected vessels
connected_vessels.equation: Pascal: P = rho*g*h constant at connection point

conservation_mass.principle: In a closed system with no reactions, total cell count is constant

conservation_energy.principle: Without heat sources/sinks, total thermal energy is constant
conservation_energy.note: Our engine has inherent dissipation from integer rounding in heat transfer

conservation_momentum.principle: In a symmetric system, net horizontal momentum should be zero
conservation_momentum.note: Symmetric sand drop should preserve zero net horizontal momentum

beverloo.note: 1-cell opening has zero Beverloo flow; CA always allows it

thermal_stratification.principle: Hot water rises above cold water (convection)
thermal_stratification.expected_ordering: temperature decreases from top to bottom

fire_spread.principle: Fire in uniform fuel propagates at roughly constant velocity
fire_spread.note: Coefficient of variation of velocity < 0.5 means roughly constant

flash_point.principle: Oil ignites faster than wood (lower activation energy)

jamming_transition.principle: Granular materials can form arches over narrow openings
jamming_transition.reference: Zuriguel et al., Physical Review Letters (2005)

graded_bedding.principle: Denser particles settle faster in fluid (Stokes' law)

domino_cascade.principle: Unsupported elements fall progressively, not instantly
domino_cascade.note: Collapse should take multiple frames (finite gravity, not teleportation)

thermal_equilibrium.principle: Hot + cold objects reach weighted-average equilibrium temperature
thermal_equilibrium.note: Same material: heat capacity cancels; equilibrium is cell-count weighted average

capillary_wicking.principle: Porous materials absorb water against gravity via capillary action
capillary_wicking.note: In our engine, dirt porosity=0.6 should absorb water, forming mud

hydrostatic_paradox.principle: Pressure at bottom depends only on height, not container shape
hydrostatic_paradox.equation: P = rho * g * h (independent of container width)

ripple_damping.principle: Surface disturbances should decay over time, not amplify
ripple_damping.mechanism: Viscous dissipation damps water surface waves

load_distribution.principle: Taller liquid column exerts more pressure at base

diffusion.principle: Heat diffuses from hot to cold, spreading over time (Fick's law)

stokes_drag.principle: Terminal velocity proportional to density difference

electrical_conductivity.note: Lightning should propagate through these elements

surface_tension.note: Higher surface tension = more cohesive droplets

sand_water.description: Sand absorbs water to become mud

non_reactive_pairs.principle: Elements without registered reactions should not transform each other

fire_oil_chain.description: Fire ignites oil, which spreads fire through connected oil

lava_water_chain.description: Lava contacts water producing steam + stone

acid_layered.description: Acid dissolves materials at rates proportional to probability

corrosion_resistance_ordering.principle: Higher acid probability = less corrosion resistant

fire_cycle.description: Wood ignites from fire/lava, burns, produces smoke

water_cycle_reaction.trigger: lava or fire heats water above boil point
water_cycle_reaction.description: Water becomes steam when heated, steam rises and may condense

reaction_mass.principle: Reactions that produce non-empty products preserve cell count

reaction_temperature.principle: Reactions with temperature constraints only fire within range
reaction_temperature.note: Current built-in rules have no temp constraints; test framework validates the mechanism

settling_timing.principle: Elements settle after 3 consecutive stable frames

momentum_symmetry.principle: Two identical elements dropped from same height at symmetric x positions should land at same y

clock_bit.principle: Elements are processed exactly once per step via clock-bit toggle

velocity_on_impact.principle: Velocity resets to 0 on landing (hitting solid surface)

drag_comparison.principle: Elements fall slower through fluid than air due to density displacement

projectile_motion.principle: Horizontal position advances linearly, vertical follows free-fall

stack_pressure.principle: Stack of elements should remain stable once settled, no element left behind

pile_stability.principle: Large granular pile should reach equilibrium (all cells settled) after enough frames
